name: Marwan Elgendy
current_status: Senior technical engineer with 15+ years building scalable backends, data platforms, and cloud-native systems.
summary: Marwan designs and delivers production systems at enterprise scale: AWS and Kubernetes platforms, distributed microservices, large-scale data processing (Spark, EMR, Databricks), and correctness-critical migrations. He has led end-to-end initiatives across architecture, infrastructure automation, and mentoring, with recent depth at IBM (Apptio), JumpCloud, and HP Instant Ink, plus earlier roles including AT&T.
strengths: Cloud-native and event-driven architecture; Domain-Driven Design for bounded contexts
High-volume and financial-grade data pipelines with reconciliation-driven cutovers
Multi-tenant APIs and hybrid data stores (PostgreSQL plus MongoDB) at tens of thousands of tenants
AWS serverless and workflow automation (Lambda, Step Functions, SQS, SNS, DynamoDB)
Monolith-to-microservices evolution while protecting availability and reliability
Cross-functional leadership with distributed teams (engineering, DevOps, QA, product)
tech_at_glance: AWS (Lambda, Step Functions, SQS, SNS, DynamoDB, API Gateway, SAM)
speaking_points: At HP Instant Ink Marwan planned and executed scaling the subscription service from thousands of customers to millions, globally, by transforming a Ruby on Rails monolith into cloud-native microservices and data pipelines using Domain-Driven Design and Test-Driven Development — completed in a matter of months across four geographically distributed teams with service reliability maintained throughout.

At Apptio he migrated Kubernetes and Java workloads to EMR Spark with Scala to solve a scaling and cost problem — pipelines were running for days and getting expensive. The hard part was correctness: financial datasets have zero tolerance for drift. He built a reconciliation pipeline that ran both systems in parallel and flagged discrepancies, which gave the team confidence to cut over incrementally rather than all at once. The result was roughly 10x faster processing with zero correctness drift.

At JumpCloud he owned end-to-end technical design and delivery of a GraphQL-based Microsoft M365 integration for more than 50,000 tenants: multi-tenant schema and resolvers, API layer, and hybrid Postgres plus MongoDB to balance read-heavy relational access with high write volume.

Marwan works with AI-assisted tooling daily: planning large initiatives and generating documentation with Claude, prototyping and writing code in Cursor, and using multi-model workflows to move faster without lowering the quality bar. This portfolio's chat agent is itself an example of that practice.
contact_preference: The contact form on this site is the preferred channel. Marwan reads every submission.
